transcript: So, um, they were very particular about me not tripping on those steps, so I kind of solved the first problem, I got here standing up. Um, I'm one of the co-founders of Figure, and I'm going to give you a little bit of background on Figure and then I want to talk about how it's intersecting with the Solana ecosystem and why that matters. Um, we started Figure back in 2018 with the idea that capital markets are going to be massively transformed by blockchain. The ability to create native digital assets where we can displace trust with truth changes the way that we trade assets, changes the way that assets are financed, um, and has a significant ramification to a lot of the marketplaces that we work through. And we were looking for a tangible use case to bring to the market, and we said, well, why don't we originate loans, aggregate them, securitize them on blockchain? We felt that we could save 85 basis points of cost in doing that. And we went to a bunch of banks and pitched this idea, and universally every bank said, this is awesome, we love it, we'd like to be the 10th bank to do this. And so it was clear no one wanted to be a first mover on it. So we stood up our lending platform and started originating loans on chain. So in 2018, we did a Home Equity Line of Credit native on public blockchain. We were the second ones to do a consumer loan on chain behind MakerDAO that started in 2017. But we started originating loans on chain, and we were doing it in what I would call the classic Fintech 2.0 way, which is we'd find the customer, we'd originate the loan, we'd use our balance sheet. But we were getting some sufficient efficiencies on the blockchain side. And very quickly we went from that direct-to-consumer model to business-to-business-to-consumer. So we now have over 250 partners that use our technology to originate loans native on public chain, including 10 of the top 20 mortgage companies. And to date, we've done, I think about $22 billion of on-chain loan origination and running at about $1.1 billion a month right now. We were the first to securitize loans on blockchain back in 2020. We were the first to do a AAA-rated securitization in '23. Um, if you go to CoinGecko, Figure HELOC I think is the 12th largest crypto asset on there. Um, and it's really demonstrating our role in the RWA ecosystem, and something that we're very committed to. And the logical extension of this is, well, how does this intersect with Solana? And it does across two very important points, and then one that I think is going to be built into over the next year. But the first is, as we were going through this process, we needed a mechanism to settle transactions on chain. We needed an ability to express dollars. And I was working with a group of banks back in '23 to create a tokenized version of deposits. And unfortunately, the regulators at that time came and said, look, we're not going to let you do this. Blockchain is not consistent with safety and soundness in the banking system. And so I went and started an S-1 process with the SEC to create a blockchain-native public equity security that pays interest but has stable value. So effectively a security version of stablecoin. And earlier this year, we went effective with this. So this is the first public security, US public security, native on public chain. And we were minting this on Provenance, which is one of the blockchains that we use for a lot of the work that we do. But we're extending that and we're now minting that Yield security, which is what it's called, native on Solana. And this is important for a couple of reasons. One, it gives the builder community a yielding version of stablecoin that, while it's a security, is still freely transferable peer-to-peer. So it does everything that a USDC or USDT would do, but it's paying interest. So there's huge value in that. More importantly, it future-proofs the risk of fiat on and off ramps. So one of the things that a lot of us remember from a year and a half ago, although it seems like so long ago, you know, we were down to maybe two banks that we could use to get fiat on and off blockchain. And with Yields as a security, we future-proof that construct. So we're effectively providing a mechanism where, irrespective of regime changes, we're going to be able to get money on and off public blockchain. So very valuable in that regard. The second, and more interesting one for today's conversation, is bringing our assets over to blockchain. So today we have about $15 billion of mortgages, a little over a billion dollars of crypto-backed loans, and other yielding assets that we want to bring into the DeFi ecosystem. So one of the things that we launched a week ago was HASTRA. And HASTRA is a liquid staking protocol where I'm effectively buying yields, or a version of wrapped yields, I'm pledging it into HASTRA for a PRIME token. That PRIME token is investing in a DeFi marketplace against our Home Equity Line of Credits. And you're taking that PRIME token onto Kamino where it's getting multiplied, or onto Raydium where it's trading. And I had to bring my phone up to get the exact numbers, but we launched this a week ago. We've got over $75 million deployed across wrapped yields and PRIME. It's the fourth biggest market on Kamino right now. Um, users are earning over 24% on their multiply product through it. And just today, Gauntlet officially announced they're allocating a portion of its cash vault to PRIME. And so what people are getting out of this is underlying yield that's coming from something they can get their arms around, right? It's not selling the forward to spot and earning the carry. It's not any algorithmic function. It's actually US mortgages. So US mortgages that are originated native on blockchain, generating yield that ultimately propagates through to support the DeFi ecosystem. The last thing that I want to talk about that we're doing, that ultimately I think is going to have a significant impact in the Solana ecosystem, is we did an S-1, we went public back in September. So Figure is a public company. Uh, but about a week and a half ago, we released another S-1 to do effectively a second IPO. We're issuing a new version of Figure equity native on public blockchain. And so this is not going to sit at DTCC. It's not going to trade on the Nasdaq or the NYSE. It's not going to work through introducing brokers like Robinhood. It's not going to rely on prime brokerage like Goldman Sachs. This security is native on blockchain. It trades on our Alternative Trading System, which is effectively a decentralized exchange. It's a self-custody, self-settle, self-clear marketplace. And you will take that security, you'll be able to attach to that ATS through a wallet rather than an introducing broker. You'll be able to take that security onto DeFi and borrow against it, lend it out. So if you look at it side-by-side, we're basically disrupting the entire equity stack and bringing it native to public chain. And one of the focus points that we have is not only bringing that equity over to the Solana ecosystem, but allowing for native Solana equity issuance as well. So we're super excited to be integrating and leaning into the community here. We're excited about what the builder community is going to be able to do with Yields as a security version of stablecoin. We're excited about the growth we've had with HASTRA, and, you know, we have billions of assets to bring over to support that growth. But I'm most excited about disrupting this ecosystem on the equity side and doing that within the Solana community as well. So thank you very much, appreciate the time.

# UPDATE -- Figure brings $YLDS to Solana, unlocking real RWA utility for DeFi
## $YLDS will deliver fiat on/off ramp capabilities and a yielding stablecoin to the Solana ecosystem and sets the stage for Solana-native Figure RWA applications
December 10, 2025 18:16 ET| Source:[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/FT%20Intermediate%CE%B4%20Inc%C2%A7)FollowFigure Technology Solutions, Inc.
Share
* * * * * * * * NEW YORK, Dec. 10, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=SO5xFwh7zHFksPIXn7Pdorr_5b5OQuSqtCkWN5JlR6dcPuNhmYdzaZKo1OGQZO9px-rnY1nNR9Eu16pI7IgI4u0HYd3HQKnjXr2qXKs2hJXdvurllMpI_xPaPqt8p_T9)(Nasdaq: FIGR) today announced that its subsidiary, Figure Certificate Company (FCC), intends to begin minting $YLDS, a registered public debt security, natively on Solana. $YLDS is a security-version of stablecoin, designed to maintain a fixed dollar price and provide a continuous yield backed by U.S. Treasuries and Treasury repo agreements.[Exponent Finance](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=u5lnKYK8LdDZ1BsWO35PRW-u_p1f3reUUUWEm_S-kvq15rqTX1XklDgUPbT6KumIq7FzcS_UVV-uC4kCzwffPmcadYERCl40aSvcJU_5Xuo=), a decentralized finance yield exchange platform on Solana, intends to be the first user of $YLDS.
The move marks the beginning of a broader, long-term ambition between Figure and Provenance Blockchain Foundation to build on Solana. As part of its efforts to promote real-world asset applications in DeFi, Figure intends to work with Provenance Blockchain to allow Solana users to access yield from a variety of Figure assets, including loans. Over the coming weeks, Figure expects to expand this collaboration with additional Solana-native integrations and initiatives that further demonstrate its leadership in bringing compliant, decentralized finance to scale.
“We are thrilled to partner with Exponent Finance as a first mover in bringing $YLDS to the Solana ecosystem,” said Mike Cagney, Figure’s co-founder and executive chairman. “We see this as a way to future-proof fiat rails onto Solana while bringing an SEC-registered yielding stablecoin to the ecosystem.”
Builders on Solana will be able to access $YLDS for payment, cross-border remit, yield and settlement applications, including directly settling SOL trades with $YLDS. In the spirit of the partnership, Figure has begun accepting SOL as collateral for crypto-backed loans with the intent to expand this to locked SOL and adopt Figure’s liquidation protection option for SOL-backed loans.
“Real-world assets (RWAs) on Solana are quickly approaching $1B, as everything from traditional assets to real estate and more are tokenized on onchain," said Lily Liu, President, Solana Foundation. "Figure’s integration builds on that momentum and demonstrates how regulated, yield-generating assets can enhance utility across the Solana ecosystem.”
Figure believes $YLDS will deliver three key advantages to the Solana ecosystem:
* **Real-world utility:**Unlike theoretical RWA concepts, $YLDS offers immediate, practical applications. The yield-bearing stablecoin, backed primarily by U.S. Treasuries, is already powering Figure's live Democratized Prime product on the Provenance Blockchain and will soon integrate with Exponent Finance's yield exchange protocol on Solana, demonstrating tangible DeFi utility from

# Figure’s marketplace accelerates with $2.5B in Q3 volume
HELOC demand, new product categories and $1.1 billion from Figure Connect helped drive results
November 14, 2025, 4:25pm*by[Flávia Furlan Nunes](https://www.housingwire.com/author/flavia-furlan-nunes/)*
[News](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/figure-technology-q3-loan-volume-2025/)\>[LendingLife](https://www.housingwire.com/category/lendinglife/)
![Earnings Season Company Reports Stock Market Ticker Words 3d Illustration](https://www.housingwire.com/wp-content/uploads/2020/02/earnings.jpeg?w=1010)
## Article Summary
Figure Technology Solutions reached $2.5 billion in Q3 2025 loan volume, up 70% year over year. The growth was fueled by HELOCs, new loan categories and growth in its blockchain-native Figure Connect platform.*AI Summary*
**Figure Technology Solutions**reached $2.5 billion in consumer loan marketplace volume in the third quarter of 2025 —a 70% year-over-year increase, the company reported Thursday in its first[earnings](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/q3-2025-earnings-mortgage-real-estate-companies/)release since going public.
Most of Figure’s volume continues to come from home equity lines of credit (HELOCs). New product categories —including[crypto-backed loans](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/cryptocurrency-mortgage-lending-umortgage-non-qm-loan/), small and medium business loans, debt-service-coverage ratio ([DSCR](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/figure-ai-dscr-loans/)) loans,[HELOCs for seniors](https://www.housingwire.com/articles/longbridge-financial-heloc-for-seniors-reverse-mortgage/)and interest-only mortgages —contributed $80 million during the quarter.
The company also nearly tripled the volume for its first-lien HELOC compared to a year earlier.
CEO Michael Tannenbaum told analysts on Friday that Figure’s marketplace model “extends beyond home equity and into the broader consumer ecosystem, capturing a larger share of the housing finance value chain.”
Third-quarter totals include $1.1 billion from[Figure Connect](https://ww

# Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana
December 04, 2025 09:00 ET| Source:[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/en/search/organization/FT%20Intermediate%CE%B4%20Inc%C2%A7)FollowFigure Technology Solutions, Inc.
Share
* * * * * * * * ![Figure and Leading Crypto Partners Launch RWA Consortium for Onchain Finance on Solana](https://ml.globenewswire.com/Resource/Download/38b97e43-d99f-4a08-9e38-3577453d7f24/rwa-consortium.png)[](#)
*New coalition advances Figure’s vision of moving capital markets to blockchain, expands real-world yield to Solana, and sets the standard for RWA collaboration across chains*
SAN FRANCISCO, Dec. 04, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) --[Figure Technology Solutions, Inc.](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=sVrR0nCGbyoSGqLpmp3W226XOcDwCfx8V3EteYCwLGxXEo0igM8ivJlwMreqY9enDApKlqpkq2h9LGXlpppDneFNwYs_wf55flepAb6tunwEmh-xksGmM2ldyKQcRF4H)(“Figure”)(Nasdaq: FIGR), the leading blockchain-native capital marketplace for the origination, funding, sale, and trading of on-chain loan products and tokenized real-world assets (RWAs), today announced the launch of an RWA consortium, a landmark coalition that will expand access to Figure's more than $1 billion in monthly on-chain loan originations, starting on Solana. The consortium represents a coordinated effort by leading crypto platforms to support and drive adoption of PRIME, the liquid staking token built on the[Hastra liquidity protocol](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=4qB14oW0HW3wV6G7eIeftmMVA8az3rMOK8PAa0Y2-BRXRv-AIPxXC8jou5QwqZswqZL167DOJEXxgNXklTx7X5zNuk4nN-6TXUv6pOrCjxitPRgZlNKhBddz_5Xu1T9Hu1XH4kRiKqkttkiJeicO8MN-CNu8gmEyyquNATcxaPNC56uSFgFkmxuV7i1s-QvGo4s2-XxOi1gshATrL0nmDL5c78zLpeU5MWvBvHd1TKM=), developed in partnership with Figure and the Provenance Blockchain Foundation. PRIME is powered by the Democratized Prime decentralized lending protocol on the Provenance Blockchain, which allows users to lend against pools of tokenized Figure RWA loans. Through this coalition, everyday DeFi users can now access institutional-grade yields previously reserved for banks and accredited investors.
The RWA Consortium builds on Figure’s vision to bring capital markets on-chain, a goal most recently reinforced during its IPO and the expansion of its RWA ecosystem across multiple blockchains. By extending beyond the Provenance Blockchain to integrate with Layer 1 networks like Solana, Figure is partnering with Hastra to leverage Chainlink[CCIP](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=AoAEgzHaPbSD8H81yfVKVDXA32VlG1HlMnUL10_xbh1thIeREQF8WzIHuEqW8sPg7s78VxMYQfWySLEd-xz4Tg==)to power the cross-chain interoperability needed for a connected, multi-chain finance ecosystem. With over $19 billion in on-chain loan originations to date and a[70% market share](https://www.globenewswire.com/Tracker?data=_8YSDjYvrN99A8tUsqQH01Y3JxsV6a895BAreWpCDDEb82mue5e6XzzI_TsT7oYru-CPY3g5Xe0uEA769WrIxfR1KhHLCDiIcXYiXdT8nwu4hb9Amh8nUkhSFupuvO2d)in the RWA private credit, Figure is uniquely positioned to bridge traditional credit markets with DeFi.
This initiative follows the recent debut of Hastra, a liquidity protocol incubated by Figure and Provenance Blockchain that allows the yields generated in Democratized Prime to reach decentralized finance, with all Hastra yield products secured by the Chainlink data standard. Hastra acts as a

I included the text '$15B RWA' as a glowing projection on a concrete wall to ground the abstract concept with the massive portfolio size mentioned in the headline, making it a diegetic part of this new architectural/data environment.

The mood is one of quiet, powerful innovation – the digital transformation of a foundational real-world asset.

editorial.headline: Figure Bridges $15B RWA Portfolio to Solana DeFi Protocols
editorial.standfirst: By integrating its SEC-registered $YLDS token and HASTRA liquid staking protocol, Figure is bypassing traditional banking rails to inject institutional mortgage yield directly into Solana. The move effectively future-proofs fiat on-ramps while offering DeFi users access to a $22 billion origination engine.
key_takeaways: Rapid DeFi Adoption: The HASTRA protocol attracted $75 million in TVL within one week of launch, becoming the fourth largest market on Kamino.
Institutional Validation: Risk management firm Gauntlet has officially allocated a portion of its cash vault to the PRIME token.
Infrastructure Disruption: Figure filed an S-1 for a blockchain-native 'Second IPO' that bypasses the DTCC and Nasdaq to trade directly on a self-custody ATS.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The convergence of Real World Assets (RWA) and high-velocity DeFi has moved from theoretical pilots to substantial liquidity events. **Figure** is leveraging its $22 billion loan origination history to aggressively integrate with the Solana ecosystem, effectively using the chain as a settlement layer for its $15 billion mortgage book. Unlike previous RWA attempts that isolated assets in permissioned silos, Figure is piping yield directly into public DeFi protocols. The immediate impact is visible in the **HASTRA** liquid staking protocol, which deployed $75 million across wrapped yields and the PRIME token within seven days of launch, offering **Kamino** users yields exceeding 24% via its multiply product.

Central to this strategy is the introduction of **$YLDS** (Yields) to Solana, an SEC-registered public debt security that functions as a yield-bearing stablecoin. This is a strategic infrastructure play designed to "future-proof" fiat rails against banking sector instability. By utilizing a registered security that is freely transferable peer-to-peer, Figure provides a compliant mechanism for on/off-ramping that remains resilient to banking regime changes or "Choke Point" style regulatory pressure. For institutional investors, this represents a stabilized, legally robust bridge between off-chain capital and on-chain execution.

The implications extend beyond yield farming into the fundamental structure of equity markets. Following its traditional IPO in September, Figure has filed an S-1 for a "Second IPO" consisting of equity issued natively on public blockchain. This issuance bypasses the **DTCC**, **Nasdaq**, and prime brokers entirely, relying instead on a self-custody, self-clearing Alternative Trading System (ATS). By bringing this equity stack to Solana, Figure is not merely tokenizing assets but displacing the entire post-trade settlement layer of traditional finance, allowing equity to be utilized as collateral within DeFi protocols with the same frictionlessness as a native token.
